what_it_does: Declares the constitution for the file-discovered mark-type registry: it defines what a mark and a mark-type are, the required MARK_TYPE schema, the live set of analysis and interaction mark-types, and the rules for adding, discovering, reading, and extending mark-types.
observations: Mark-types are directory-discovered, one self-registering Python file per kind, mirroring the roles/skills/projections/node-types registries.
A mark-type is declared as a module-level MARK_TYPE dict with id, value_shape, direction, and desc; malformed entries fail loudly at discovery.
Direction is either surface (positive signal) or subtract (noise to subtract); ai_fingerprint is the seed exercising the surface-vs-subtract split.

Lists 13 live mark-types: six analysis types (gold_likelihood, ai_fingerprint, contradiction, built_twice, overlap, strain) and seven interaction/operator types (comment, reaction, favour, decision_take, decision_retract, decision_update, decision_update_accept).
Render-not-judge principle: marks describe dispositions; judgement and resolution happen in later reduce passes or operator overrule.
The file requires itself to be updated whenever a mark-type is added; tests/mark_types_acceptance.py asserts the live set matches this document.
Decision-related mark-types model a lightweight proposal/accept/reject/undo lifecycle for decision cards, with append-only, audit-preserving semantics.
Mark-passes append findings; reading uses subtractive/as_records pure READS and no mark-pass resolves.
standouts: File-discovered registry: adding a mark-type means adding a file, not editing a central dict.
Surface vs. subtract direction vocabulary, with ai_fingerprint as the inversion example.
Decision-surface interaction marks (take/retract/update/accept) form a small append-only, audit-preserving operator/AI co-editing protocol.
Self-referential requirement that the constitution must be updated for every new mark-type and is enforced by acceptance tests.
conventions: Uses constitution front-matter with type, register, module, aliases, tags, governs, relates-to, and status.
Mirrors the ONE registry mechanism used by roles/skills/projections/node-types.
Declares a module-level MARK_TYPE dict per file with id matching the file stem.
Follows render-not-judge / read-only floor: mark-types are data, not actions.
Append-only, audit-preserving semantics for operator interaction marks.
Breaks the typical central-dictionary pattern by requiring directory discovery and file-per-entry.
concerns: The live-set list is manually maintained in this file and must be kept in sync with directory contents; drift risk if a file is added without updating the constitution.
Decision marks have nuanced lifecycle rules (e.g., accepted options-touching update re-opens a decided card) noted as 'Hole-1' but not fully resolved here.
Several mark-type value shapes are described informally (claim, span, free) without a formal schema shown in this file.
The 'To extend' section references a future create_mark_type / runtime/authoring.py seam flagged as a separate coordinated pass, leaving wiring unspecified.
notes: This is the constitution for the mark-type vocabulary: it lists every kind of disposition a cognition pass may write, how each is read, and the rules for extending the registry without editing code.
questions: What is the exact file path and discovery glob used by MarkTypeRegistry?
How is the value_shape vocabulary validated beyond the open list (score/label/bool/span/free/claim)?
What is the canonical decision address format and how does runtime/decision_registry.compose_state resolve timestamps across marks?
How does compose_definition match a decision_update_accept to its corresponding decision_update (by ts only, or also by target/field/value)?
What happens when a mark-type file is malformed at discovery — does it fail the whole registry load or skip the file?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be the constitution for the mark-type registry; actual content matches, listing definitions, guarantees, the live set, extension rules, and seams.
apparent_themes: Registry-as-data: vocabulary is declared, discovered, and versioned in files rather than hardcoded.
Render-not-judge: surface findings without resolving them.
Subtractive inversion: denoising is a first-class direction.
Operator-in-the-loop: interaction marks support lightweight, audit-preserving co-editing.
Self-auditing documentation: acceptance tests enforce that the constitution reflects the live registry.
intention_signals: Establish a stable, extensible vocabulary for cognitive dispositions that can be added by dropping a file, while keeping marks descriptive and deferring judgement to later passes and the operator.
novelty: Treats mark-types as a file-discovered registry of dispositions with a built-in surface/subtract direction, and embeds a lightweight append-only decision lifecycle (take/retract/update/accept) inside the same mark vocabulary.
contribution: Provides a declared, file-discovered vocabulary for mark-types and a clear floor rule (render-not-judge), enabling mark-passes to write findings in a consistent, extensible, operator-overridable way.
summary_for_embedding: Constitution for the file-discovered mark-type registry defining marks as dispositions, the MARK_TYPE schema, the live set of analysis and interaction mark-types, and rules for registry extension and render-not-judge reading.
intention_for_embedding: To stabilize and document the vocabulary of dispositions that cognition passes may write over corpus units, making the registry extensible by adding files while keeping marks descriptive and judgement deferred.
